projectDescription: A Windows security vulnerability proof-of-concept that abuses Windows Defender's cloud-tag file rewriting behavior to overwrite system files and escalate privileges to SYSTEM.
keyFeatures: Abuses Windows Defender cloud-tag file restoration behavior
Uses Cloud Files API (CldApi) to create placeholder files with cloud sync tags
Leverages Volume Shadow Copy service for TOCTOU race condition
Exploits batch oplock (FSCTL_REQUEST_BATCH_OPLOCK) for precise timing control
Sets directory reparse point to redirect writes to System32
Achieves arbitrary write to C:\Windows\System32 as a low-privilege user
Escalates to SYSTEM by replacing TieringEngineService.exe and triggering COM instantiation
Named pipe trick to spawn console in the original user session after privilege escalation
publicApiSurface: main() — entry point; orchestrates the full exploit chain
ShadowCopyFinderThread() — monitors object manager directory for new VSS volumes
DoCloudStuff() — registers Cloud Files sync root and creates placeholder with cloud tag
RetrieveCurrentVSSList() — enumerates existing HarddiskVolumeShadowCopy devices via NtQueryDirectoryObject
LaunchTierManagementEng() — triggers COM instantiation of StorageTier engine (GUID 50d185b9-...)
LaunchConsoleInSessionId() — spawns conhost.exe in the original user session via named pipe session ID lookup
IsRunningAsLocalSystem() — checks if current token is NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM and spawns console if so
rev() — reverses the EICAR test string to evade static detection at compile time
